cards.title: Nativ Wraps MLX in a Native Mac AI Desktop App

panels.head: LM Studio has a new Mac-native rival
panels.sub: Nativ is a macOS desktop app that runs AI models locally via Apple's MLX framework — no cloud, no API keys, no latency tax.

panels.head: From MLX-VLM to a full desktop shell
panels.sub: Prince Canuma, author of the MLX-VLM Python library for vision-LLMs on Apple Silicon, wrapped that same stack into a native GUI app.

panels.head: Chat UI + localhost API server, out of the box
panels.sub: Like LM Studio, Nativ ships both a conversational interface and a local API endpoint — drop-in compatible with tools that already speak OpenAI-style HTTP.

panels.head: Auto-discovers models already in your Hugging Face cache
panels.sub: If you've previously pulled MLX models via the HF CLI, Nativ finds them without any manual import step — zero friction to get running.

panels.head: A faster on-ramp for offline, air-gapped AI prototyping
panels.sub: For PMs building AI features in regulated or bandwidth-constrained environments, a local API server on a MacBook is a cheap way to prototype and demo without touching production infrastructure.

cards.title: One app that remembers who likes what

panels.head: Remembering what 30 people like is a part-time job
panels.sub: Birthdays, preferences, past gifts — it all lives in your head until it doesn't. The result: generic gifts, last-minute panic, or nothing at all.

panels.head: Anyone with a large social or family graph
panels.sub: Particularly acute for people in large extended families or tight friend groups where gifting is a social norm. Recurs 5–15 times a year per person.

panels.head: A persistent people-CRM built around gift occasions
panels.sub: You enter each person once: birthday, interests, size, price range. The app surfaces timely nudges and generates ranked gift links — no re-entry, no cross-app juggling.

panels.head: Finder and Giftster are catalogues, not assistants
panels.sub: They require repeated manual input per occasion and offer generic suggestions. Neither maintains a living profile per person that improves over time.

panels.head: Search r/SomebodyMakeThis and r/GiftIdeas for duplicate posts
panels.sub: Estimate post frequency and upvote counts as a proxy for demand. If the pattern repeats monthly, build a waitlist landing page with one onboarding question: 'How many people do you buy gifts for each year?'

cards.title: Inside Claude Code: 65% of PRs, 80% Smaller Prompts

panels.head: Claude Tag closes 65% of PRs for its own dev team
panels.sub: Anthropic's Slack-native coding agent is now the primary code contributor for the Claude Code product team — eating its own homework at scale.

panels.head: Claude Code ships to Anthropic employees first
panels.sub: Features only reach external users if they show measurable retention with the internal cohort — a tight dogfooding loop Anthropic calls 'ant fooding'.

panels.head: The Claude Code system prompt shrank by 80%
panels.sub: Packed examples and 'don't do X' lists were best practice for older models, but actively hurt quality on Fable 5 and Opus 4.8. Leaner prompts now outperform exhaustive ones.

panels.head: Auto mode changed the human role, not just the output
panels.sub: The team went from approving every file touch to delegating full implementation and redirecting their attention to product experience decisions. One-shotting full features with Fable is now routine.

panels.head: The right response to agent productivity isn't doing more of the same
panels.sub: The Claude Code team's advice: raise the quality bar, not just the throughput. If an agent can implement it in hours, the PM's job is to set more ambitious targets — not to ship the same work faster.

cards.title: Italian startup ORiS bets on laser power-beaming

panels.head: What if you could refuel a satellite with a laser?
panels.sub: ORiS, an Italian startup, is building wireless power-beaming tech that transmits energy to spacecraft using focused laser light — no physical contact required.

panels.head: €5 million seed round closed
panels.sub: The €5M (~$5.7M) raise gives ORiS runway to advance its laser power-beaming system from concept toward hardware demonstration. Round size is modest, suggesting early TRL.

panels.head: Lasers as power lines in orbit
panels.sub: Power-beaming converts electricity to a laser beam, transmits it across space, and converts it back on the receiving spacecraft. It could extend satellite lifetimes or enable low-battery edge-orbit assets to receive top-ups from a dedicated beaming platform.

panels.head: In-space power is a crowded but early race
panels.sub: Competitors include Virtus Solis, Solaren, and JAXA's long-running SBSP programme. Laser-based beaming competes with microwave approaches — lasers offer tighter beams but are more sensitive to pointing accuracy and atmospheric interference for ground applications.

panels.head: Watch for a hardware milestone, not the raise
panels.sub: At €5M, ORiS is pre-demonstration. The signal that matters will be an in-orbit power-transfer test — that's when the technology moves from company target to demonstrated result.
